examples.title: Algorithm: SLR Parsing Table Construction
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating the complete SLR parsing table construction process including LR(0) items, closure, goto, and table generation
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ConstructSLRTable
INPUT: Grammar G with productions
OUTPUT: SLR Parsing Table (ACTION and GOTO tables)

BEGIN
 // Step 1: Augment the grammar
 Add new start production S' → S
 Let productions = all productions including S' → S

 // Step 2: Compute FOLLOW sets for all non-terminals
 FOLLOW = ComputeFOLLOW(productions)

 // Step 3: Build canonical collection of LR(0) item sets
 C = BuildCanonicalCollection(productions)

 // Step 4: Build SLR parsing table
 FOR each state I in C DO
 state_index = index of I in C

 // Compute GOTO for each grammar symbol
 FOR each symbol X in grammar symbols DO
 goto_set = GOTO(I, X)
 IF goto_set is not empty THEN
 j = index of goto_set in C
 IF X is terminal THEN
 ACTION[state_index, X] = "shift " + j
 ELSE // X is non-terminal
 GOTO[state_index, X] = j
 END IF
 END IF
 END FOR

 // Handle reduce actions
 FOR each item [A → α•] in I DO
 IF A is S' (start symbol) AND α = S THEN
 ACTION[state_index, $] = "accept"
 ELSE
 FOR each terminal a in FOLLOW(A) DO
 ACTION[state_index, a] = "reduce " + A + " → " + α
 END FOR
 END IF
 END FOR
 END FOR

 RETURN ACTION, GOTO
END

ALGORITHM ComputeFOLLOW
INPUT: Grammar productions
OUTPUT: FOLLOW sets for all non-terminals
BEGIN
 Initialize FOLLOW(S') = {$}
 Initialize FOLLOW(other non-terminals) = empty set

 REPEAT
 FOR each production A → α DO
 // Case 1: A → αBβ
 FOR each position where B appears before terminal β DO
 Add FIRST(β) - {ε} to FOLLOW(B)
 END FOR

 // Case 2: A → αB or A → αBβ where FIRST(β) contains ε
 IF B is at end OR FIRST(β) contains ε THEN
 Add FOLLOW(A) to FOLLOW(B)
 END IF
 END FOR
 UNTIL no changes to any FOLLOW set

 RETURN FOLLOW
END

ALGORITHM BuildCanonicalCollection
INPUT: Grammar productions
OUTPUT: Collection of LR(0) item sets
BEGIN
 // Create initial item set
 I0 = CLOSURE({[S' → •S]})
 C = {I0}

 // Iterate to find all states
 REPEAT
 FOR each state I in C DO
 FOR each grammar symbol X DO
 goto_I_X = GOTO(I, X)
 IF goto_I_X is not empty AND goto_I_X not in C THEN
 Add goto_I_X to C
 END IF
 END FOR
 END FOR
 UNTIL no new states added

 RETURN C
END

ALGORITHM CLOSURE
INPUT: Set of items I
OUTPUT: Closure of I
BEGIN
 CLOSURE_I = I

 REPEAT
 FOR each item [A → α•Bβ] in CLOSURE_I DO
 FOR each production B → γ DO
 Add [B → •γ] to CLOSURE_I
 END FOR
 END FOR
 UNTIL no new items added

 RETURN CLOSURE_I
END

ALGORITHM GOTO
INPUT: Set of items I, grammar symbol X
OUTPUT: GOTO(I, X)
BEGIN
 J = empty set

 FOR each item [A → α•Xβ] in I DO
 Add [A → αX•β] to J
 END FOR

 RETURN CLOSURE(J)
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode outlines the complete SLR parsing table construction process: (1) First, augment the grammar with S' → S, (2) Compute FOLLOW sets for all non-terminals using the standard rules, (3) Build the canonical collection of LR(0) item sets using closure and goto operations, (4) Finally, populate the ACTION and GOTO tables based on the item sets and FOLLOW information.

examples.title: Python: Complete SLR Parsing Table Construction
examples.description: Full Python implementation demonstrating construction of SLR parsing tables with LR(0) items, FOLLOW sets, and the ACTION/GOTO table generation
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates the complete SLR parsing table construction: (1) Grammar preprocessing with augmentation, (2) FIRST set computation, (3) FOLLOW set computation using the iterative algorithm, (4) Building the canonical collection of LR(0) item sets using closure and goto operations, (5) Finally building the ACTION and GOTO tables with proper shift/reduce/accept actions. The implementation handles the expression grammar from the topic content and prints the complete parsing tables and item sets.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n³) for FOLLOW computation, O(n²) for canonical collection
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n²) for storing all states and items

examples.title: C: SLR Parsing Table Construction
examples.description: C implementation showing LR(0) item generation, FOLLOW set computation, and parsing table construction for compiler design
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates the SLR parsing table construction: (1) Grammar is initialized with the expression grammar (E→E+T|T, T→T*F|F, F→(E)|id), (2) LR(0) items are generated with dot positions, (3) The closure operation adds items for non-terminals after the dot, (4) GOTO operation computes state transitions, (5) FOLLOW sets are computed based on grammar rules, (6) The canonical collection of LR(0) item sets is built, and finally (7) The ACTION and GOTO tables are populated with shift, reduce, and accept actions. The code shows how shift actions are added when the dot is before a terminal, and reduce actions are added based on FOLLOW sets when the dot is at the end of a production.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n³) worst case for closure computation with n productions
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n²) for storing all states and items
